answer:
Let $x_1, x_2, \dots, x_n$ be real variables. We want to find real polynomials $p_1, p_2, \dots, p_k$ such that
\[(x_1 + x_2 + \cdots + x_n)^2 + p_1(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 + p_2(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 + \cdots + p_k(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 = n(x_1^2 + x_2^2 + \cdots + x_n^2).\]
Let $S = x_1 + x_2 + \cdots + x_n$. Then $S^2 = x_1^2 + x_2^2 + \cdots + x_n^2 + 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j$.
We have
\[ S^2 = \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 + 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j \]
We want to find $p_1, p_2, \dots, p_k$ such that
\[ S^2 + p_1(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 + p_2(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 + \cdots + p_k(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 + 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j + p_1(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 + p_2(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 + \cdots + p_k(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j + p_1(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 + p_2(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 + \cdots + p_k(x_1, \dots, x_n)^2 = (n-1) \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
Let $p_i(x_1, \dots, x_n) = 0$ for all $i$. Then we have
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 + 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j = (n-1) \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
This is not true in general.

Let $p_i(x_1, \dots, x_n) = 0$ for all $i$. Then we have
\[ (x_1 + x_2 + \cdots + x_n)^2 = n(x_1^2 + x_2^2 + \cdots + x_n^2) \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 + 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j = (n-1) \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
This is not true in general.

Consider $k=n$. Let $p_i(x_1, \dots, x_n) = x_i^2$ for $i=1, \dots, n$. Then
\[ (x_1 + x_2 + \cdots + x_n)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 + \cdots + x_i^2 = \left( \sum_{i=1}^n x_i \right)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ = \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 + 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = 2 \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 + 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j \]
We want this to be $n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2$.
So $2 \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 + 2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2$.
$2 \sum_{i < j} x_i x_j = (n-2) \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2$.

Let $k=n$. We can choose $p_i(x_1, \dots, x_n) = x_i^2$. Then
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n (x_i + \cdots + x_n)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n (x_i^2 + \cdots + x_n^2) + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ 2 \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
This is false.

Let $k=n$. We can choose $p_i(x_1, \dots, x_n) = x_i^2$. Then
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n (x_1 + \cdots + x_n)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n (x_1 + \cdots + x_n)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n \left( \sum_{j=1}^n x_j \right)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n \left( \sum_{j=1}^n x_j \right)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
Let $x_1 = x_2 = \cdots = x_n = 1$. Then
\[ (n)^2 + n = n(n) \]
\[ n^2 + n = n^2 \]
This is false.

Let $k=n$. We can choose $p_i(x_1, \dots, x_n) = x_i^2$. Then
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n (x_1 + \cdots + x_n)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n \left( \sum_{j=1}^n x_j \right)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n \left( \sum_{j=1}^n x_j \right)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
Let $x_1 = x_2 = \cdots = x_n = 1$. Then
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n (n)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n 1 = n \sum_{i=1}^n 1 \]
\[ n^3 + n = n^2 \]
This is false.

Consider $k=n$. Let $p_i(x_1, \dots, x_n) = x_i$. Then
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n (x_1 + \cdots + x_n)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n \left( \sum_{j=1}^n x_j \right)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 = n \sum_{i=1}^n x_i^2 \]
Let $x_1 = x_2 = \cdots = x_n = 1$. Then
\[ \sum_{i=1}^n (n)^2 + \sum_{i=1}^n 1 = n \sum_{i=1}^n 1 \]
\[ n^3 + n = n^2 \]
This is false.

The least natural number $k$ is $n$.

\boxed{n}

instructions:
You are a math problem solver.
